key_insights.insight: Being different in any way leads to constant reminders and microaggressions, teaching empathy for others facing similar experiences.
key_insights.quote: If you're different in any way right you're reminded of it non-stop

key_insights.insight: Repeatedly pointing out someone's difference, even with good intentions, can be exhausting and hurtful because it reinforces their self-consciousness.
key_insights.quote: If you see somebody is different they do not need to be told they do not need it pointed out every single day because everyone has told them their entire life that they're different.

key_insights.insight: Being different can be a 'c-word radar' for judging people's character, because some will react negatively out of their own insecurities.
key_insights.quote: Being different in any way whatsoever I think really teaches you about people and about the hate that's out there and about the unhappiness that's out there because that's where it all comes from.

key_insights.insight: Body shaming, whether intentional or not, can cause shame and embarrassment, impacting a person's desire to participate in social activities.
key_insights.quote: That's what people have done to me for the last 30 years they body shame me like because they've talked about my statureand I felt ashamed that's body shaming.

key_insights.insight: Labeling harmful behaviors like body shaming empowers individuals to name the offense and potentially change the narrative.
key_insights.quote: Body shaming and actually having it named you just think oh good for you and it's the younger generation you do it they're so great and they just say no come on that's body shaming

key_insights.insight: The world constantly reinforces feelings of being weird when one perceives themselves as different.
key_insights.quote: The world will not the world is not shy and letting you know that you're weird you know that there's something weird about you and certainly that's what I felt I felt weird.

actionable_advice.advice: Before making a comment about someone's difference, consider if they've heard it before.
actionable_advice.how_to: Pause and reflect if your comment is original or might be a repeated experience for the individual.

actionable_advice.advice: When interacting with someone who is different, treat them normally.
actionable_advice.how_to: Engage in conversation without drawing attention to their difference.

memorable_stories: The speaker recounts constant jokes about their height at public events, which are tiresome despite often being harmlessly intended.
The speaker describes feeling the need to hide away or explain away why they were weird, and how their ability to be funny helped them cope with it.
The speaker tells the story about when his dad left at 9 years old, contributing to the formation of a false self.
contrarian_claims: A tall person expresses a desire to be shorter, contrasting the common desire among shorter men to be taller.
The speaker argues that even seemingly harmless jokes directed at people who are different can become body shaming.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 91 - The "Harmless" Thing You Do That Hurts So Many People! - Richard Osmond
